tree.setup: You walk into a small restaurant for lunch.
tree.setup_zh: 你走进一家小餐厅吃午饭。

options.feedback: Perfect — 位 is the polite measure word for people.

options.text_zh: 一直走，左拐，右边。谢谢你！
options.text_pinyin: yìzhí zǒu, zuǒ guǎi, yòubiān. xièxie nǐ!
options.text_en: Go straight, turn left, on the right. Thank you!
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to tell them it's just you, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_en: Please go to the train station.
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to tell them it's just you, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo de, qǐng zuò. nín xiǎng chī shénme?
turns.text_en: OK, please sit. What would you like to eat?

options.text_pinyin: hǎo de, gěi wǒ lái liǎng jīn ba.
options.text_en: OK, give me two jin please.
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to order fried rice, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_zh: 谢谢！三点要见朋友，我得走了。
options.text_pinyin: xièxie! sān diǎn yào jiàn péngyou, wǒ děi zǒu le.
options.text_en: Thanks! I'm meeting a friend at three, I have to go.
options.feedback: Off-topic — you were asked to order fried rice, but this doesn't fit the conversation.

options.text_pinyin: wǒ yào yī fèn chǎofàn, xièxie.
options.text_en: I'd like one serving of fried rice, thank you.
options.feedback: Great — clear order with 份 as measure word and a thank you.

tree.cultural_note: In Chinese restaurants, 位 (wèi) is the polite measure word for guests, while 个 (gè) is informal. Using 请 (qǐng) and 谢谢 (xièxie) keeps things smooth.
